objective: Switch CleanMac to the latest remote main branch and fix the issue where the app cannot scan installed applications.
exit_condition: The goal is complete when local main is fast-forwarded to the latest origin/main, the application scanning path can discover installed apps from /Applications and user Applications directories without being blocked by app bundle handling or permissions assumptions, focused tests cover the regression, swift test passes, ./script/build_and_run.sh --verify passes, .loopgoal/state.json records the final state, and the intended changes are committed.

phases.name: Read-only investigation of app scanning paths and tests
Verify app scan repair and runtime launch

current.focus: Read-only investigation found AppUninstaller is not best-effort on real file systems: one unreadable or unusual child/support item can throw the whole app scan, symlink .app entries can be skipped when discovered as children, and wrapper-style outer .app bundles without Info.plist are not searched for nested real app bundles. Baseline swift test passed in an escalated environment after the default sandbox blocked SwiftPM manifest execution.
current.next_action: Write failing AppUninstaller regression tests, then implement the smallest best-effort scan repair.

decisions.decision: Treat this request as GOAL mode.
decisions.reason: The user asked for a result-driven fix with a clear completion condition rather than a recurring loop.

decisions.decision: Initialize the checkpoint after switching to latest main.
decisions.reason: The previous checkpoint belonged to a completed image asset task on another branch; the user explicitly asked to work from the latest remote main for this repair.

decisions.decision: Use subagents only for read-only investigation.
decisions.reason: The user-provided AGENTS instructions allow parallel subagent investigation but reserve edits, verification, and commits for the main agent.

decisions.decision: Repair AppUninstaller as a best-effort scanner rather than changing the default application roots.
decisions.reason: Both subagent investigation and main-thread checks confirmed /Applications exists and contains apps; the brittle path is per-item filesystem errors, symlink app children, and wrapper apps with nested real bundles.
This is a bug fix and the repo already has focused AppUninstaller tests where the real filesystem edge cases can be reproduced.
